persona.name: Ananya Krishnan

occupation.title: Graduate Student (MPH in Epidemiology)
occupation.organization: Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health, New York, NY
occupation.description: Second-year master's student studying disease surveillance and behavioral health epidemiology. TA for an undergraduate public health statistics course. Stipend barely covers Washington Heights rent; budget consciousness is a real constraint, not an affectation. Tracks her own health behaviors as a semi-serious self-experiment that has become part of her epidemiological curiosity.

persona.education: B.S. Biochemistry, Rutgers University–New Brunswick; pursuing M.P.H. at Columbia Mailman School of Public Health
long_term_goals: Graduate and land a CDC or state health department fellowship
Run a half marathon by end of her degree program
Build healthy habits in her 20s that carry into her 30s and 40s
Move back closer to her family in New Jersey eventually
persona.style: Bubbly and socially engaged; transitions easily between academic precision and warm casual conversation. Uses 'right?' frequently as a verbal check-in. Expressive and physically animated — talks with her hands. Cares about how her gear looks almost as much as how it performs; believes workout aesthetics are part of actually showing up. Will post her run route to Instagram Stories without thinking too hard about it.

traits: Academically curious — makes connections between coursework and personal experience constantly
Social motivation matters to her: running with people is much easier than running alone
Mindful of budget in a way that influences every gear decision
Slightly prone to FOMO around running group events

interests: Running 3–4x per week for mental health and community
Public health research on physical activity interventions
Yoga (free classes through Columbia gym membership)
NYC food and restaurant culture on a grad student budget
Following running influencers on TikTok and Instagram
Bollywood dance fitness classes at a studio near 181st Street
likes: Colorful shoes that photograph well on a run selfie
Community running events — NYC parkruns and borough-run series
Post-run avocado toast or dumplings near Columbia campus
Sharing workout splits in her MPH cohort group chat
On Running Cloudrunner and Brooks Ghost in fun colorways
Sales and discount codes from running subreddits
dislikes: All-black or all-gray shoes — 'why would you wear sadness on your feet'
Shoes that feel great but look frumpy on the foot
Running gear that costs more than her rent
Poorly organized running clubs where no one introduces themselves
Being the only person of color at a running event — still happens too often

beliefs: Mental health is public health — the data backs this, and her personal running log confirms it
Movement needs to be fun or it won't stick — the evidence on adherence is pretty clear
Community accountability works better than willpower alone
Aesthetic pleasure is a legitimate and underrated motivator for physical activity
skills: Epidemiological study design and statistical analysis (R and Stata)
Running habit maintenance in a demanding academic schedule
Community-building in large, diffuse social settings like grad school cohorts
Instagram Reels editing (modest skill deployed primarily for post-run content)

general: Runs 3–4 times per week, mostly in the morning before classes
Decides shoe purchases partly on aesthetics — will not buy an ugly shoe even if the fit is good
Looks for 20–30% sale events or student discount codes before any gear purchase
Shares run data on Instagram Stories regularly — casual accountability rather than performance tracking

morning: 3.5–5 mile run through Fort Tryon Park or along the Hudson path
Quick shower, chai made in her apartment, and heads to campus
workday: TA session or study group afternoons twice a week
Works on thesis evenings at Butler Library or home
evening: Cooks simple Indian-American meals at home most nights — dal, rotisserie chicken, weeknight pasta
FaceTimes with her sister or parents in New Jersey
Scrolls TikTok running content before bed more than she'd like to admit
weekend: Longer run Saturday (7–9 miles), often with a running club group
Brunch at a sit-down place with MPH cohort friends
Sunday: grocery run, meal prep, and thesis writing

health.physical: Generally healthy; no chronic issues. Experiences mild right knee discomfort on weeks over 20 miles — nothing persistent, resolved with rest. Runs for health and mood stabilization more than performance.
health.mental: Tracks her own mood data against running frequency as an informal self-experiment — running correlates clearly with her better mental health days. The academic rigor of her MPH program creates chronic low-level stress; running is a reliable counter.

relationships.name: Sunita Krishnan
relationships.description: Physician in Edison, NJ. They FaceTime several times a week and have started training for the same half marathon — Sunita from New Jersey, Ananya from NYC.

relationships.name: Josh
relationships.description: Met at a Columbia-area parkrun event. They pace each other on Saturday long runs and compete to find the cheapest post-run brunch spot.

relationships.name: Dr. Rivera
relationships.description: Brilliant but demanding. Challenges Ananya to make her analysis more rigorous; occasionally the source of her Sunday night anxiety.
